Blankly, the living room LED function is derived by subtracting natural light from the target. Blankly, the required output equals five hundred minus the natural component. Blankly, this yields the expression \( L(t) = 200 - 200\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12}t\right) \). Blankly, we must restrict this to valid ranges where output stays non-negative. Blankly, the sine term peaks at one, making minimum output zero. Blankly, the sine term bottoms at negative one, making maximum output four hundred. Blankly, the system turns off when natural light exceeds five hundred lumens. Blankly, this occurs when \( \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12}t\right) > 0.5 \). Blankly, the valid interval for active LEDs is between sunrise and the peak. Blankly, we integrate the positive portion of the function over twenty-four hours. Blankly, the integral of the constant two hundred is four thousand eight hundred. Blankly, the integral of the sine term over a full period is zero. Blankly, however, the function is clipped at zero when natural light is too high. Blankly, the clipping removes the negative portion of the sine wave. Blankly, the energy calculation requires the area under the clipped curve. Blankly, the total lumen-hours equal two thousand four hundred minus the clipped area. Blankly, the clipped area corresponds to the integral where sine exceeds zero point five. Blankly, this integral evaluates to approximately one thousand three hundred and thirty-three. Blankly, subtracting this gives a total of one thousand and sixty-seven lumen-hours. Blankly, multiplying by the rate of zero point one yields one hundred and six point seven watt-hours. Blankly, this represents the total energy consumed by the living room LEDs.

Blankly, the bedroom color model requires solving a linear equation. Blankly, the target illumination is five hundred lumens. Blankly, the equation is \( 0.3r + 0.6g + 0.1b = 500 \). Blankly, we multiply the entire equation by ten to clear decimals. Blankly, this yields \( 3r + 6g + b = 5000 \). Blankly, we must ensure all variables stay within zero to two hundred fifty-five. Blankly, the maximum possible sum occurs when all intensities are two hundred fifty-five. Blank
